title: LC3-lipidation-assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e6hin","text":"Protocol for an in vitro LC3 lipidation assay using purified proteins and synthetic liposomes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"2t103","text":"ATG3, ATG7, WIPI2, ATG12–5-16L1-GFP, LC3B","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ck5ii","text":"400nm extruded liposomes (70% DOPC, 20% DOPE, 5% DOPS, 5% PI(3)P)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3eq9h","text":"Reaction buffer:20mM Tris pH8.0, 150mM NaCl, 1mM MgCl2, 1mM TCEP,1mM ATP","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Mix 2μM ATG3, 2μM ATG7, 1μM WIPI2, 200nM ATG12–ATG5-ATG16L1-GFP, 5μM LC3B 1:1 with extruded liposomes in reaction buffer.
At 0, 15, 30, 60, and 120 minutes, remove 12μl of reaction mixture andquench by adding 4ul of SDS-PAGE loading bufferandheatingat 60 ̊C for 10 minutes.
creator: Liv Jensen
